title: For those that have Die Sterne and or Der Monde
display_title: For those that have Die Sterne and or Der Monde
wikitext:
[]: For those that have Die Sterne and/or Der Monde             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue May 24, 2005 1:36 pm How much did you pay for it? I'm just curious what they are going for.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Aug 24, 2005 8:36 am I don't own it, but I would caution you that I've seen a number of what I can only assume to be bootleg copies for sale. Why do I say they are bootlegged? Because I doubt you can get a real one for five bucks including shipping!

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Aug 24, 2005 8:47 am as i've said before, there's currently one for sale on amazon.co.uk for Â£130 = $240 it's rare and people take advantage of that. carl horn was supposed to be getting me one with his ........connections, but he's not been on in months...

[]: Re: For those that have Die Sterne and/or Der Monde             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Aug 24, 2005 10:48 am Digitalex wrote: How much did you pay for it? I'm just curious what they are going for.

[]: Re: For those that have Die Sterne and/or Der Monde             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Aug 24, 2005 10:58 am the_seventh_child wrote: Digitalex wrote: How much did you pay for it? I'm just curious what they are going for.

[]: Re: For those that have Die Sterne and/or Der Monde             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:17 am Digitalex wrote: I was shocked how large the books were. They are awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:20 am awwwwwwwww, i suppose it's in german, ja?

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:28 am ice reaper wrote: awwwwwwwww, i suppose it's in german, ja?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:31 am ice reaper wrote: awwwwwwwww, i suppose it's in german, ja?

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Wed Aug 24, 2005 12:40 pm Ahh, I want Der Monde. o__o; My friend has E-mono, though. We like looking through it to see all the Eva stuff we'll never be able to afford. But...Yoshiyuki Sadamoto's artwork~ <3

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Wed Aug 24, 2005 2:01 pm I bought Die Sterne from this site about two years ago. That same site is notorious for selling bootleg merchandise, however said bootlegs pretty much only entail audio CDs. I also paid $45 for it, and it is legit. The problem is that the site seems to be some kind of disposal for overstocked anime merchanise, so it's common for them to smiply run out of whatever's popular, with no reliable prospects of restocking. Right now, it seems you can get Die Sterne and Der Mond at amazon.co.jp for around $31.00 each. Of course, shipping will be a bitch that will probably level out the playing field.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu Aug 25, 2005 5:48 am Digitalex wrote: ice reaper wrote: awwwwwwwww, i suppose it's in german, ja?

[]:             Quote Post by Xenon » Thu Aug 25, 2005 12:25 pm Got my copy of Der Mond whilst on holiday in France this summer... 22 Euros! I had no idea that I got it for such a good price... I live in the UK, so it was breathtaking to see an actual piece of Eva merchandise in the shop, let alone Der Mond! They also had the soundtrack, but I didn't have enough money. :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Thu Aug 25, 2005 2:13 pm Hmmm.... 22 euros... :roll: Let's see, that's R154.00 on my side of the world, which is enough for a botlle and a half of Tennessi's finest or half a gramm.... which ever tickles your fancy.. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Thu Aug 25, 2005 6:46 pm Can I just say Titus , that it is actually R175.16. But I dont think anyone said 22 euros anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Fri Aug 26, 2005 6:25 am 22 euros would be about Â£12....... my god. you live in sussex.....i'm not brilliant with geogrophy, even though it's in my own country, but there is this cool shop in leeds called 'cibre city anime' and it's packed with anime merchandise.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Aug 26, 2005 9:37 pm I believe I got Der Mond at $42 including shipping.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 02, 2005 6:34 pm Monkey has Der Mond in English, but the bastard never scanned the translation comments in the back like he said he would! :gets the rubber bands:

[]:             Quote Post by chibi » Sun Sep 04, 2005 1:18 am I got Die Sterne in Milan, Italy for 25â‚¬ (it was a ready printed price in the book).
